summary: Russian forces are using large-caliber sniper rifles and MANPADS teams to target Ukrainian "Baba Yaga" and "Vampire" fixed-wing drones, coordinating via communication systems to monitor the "lower sky." The Russian "Rubicon" Center reported intercepting over 2,000 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators destroying 110 targets on April 11 and 191 on April 13, 2026, across multiple directions including Donbas, Belgorod, and Sumy, while coordinating with Russian Aerospace Forces. Ukrainian forces are employing FPV drones to strike Russian soldiers, including those on motorcycles or feigning surrender, and are using electronic warfare to interfere with Russian drone operations, with some units detecting incoming FPV threats using drone detectors.
